weeklyPriorities: 2nd serve success rate — topspin + sidespin, keep it in
Overhead — run behind the ball, lift racket after the ball drops slightly
Approaching shots — anticipate speed, prepare then move; aim service box; first volley deep not a winner

weeklyOverview.focus: Volley; approaching shots for slow and high balls; 2nd serves
weeklyOverview.drill: Rally, double games, volley at net, single games, serving, ball machine volley

focus.headline: Volley — especially backhand
focus.body: This week in Notion you're prioritizing backhand volley, second-serve consistency, overhead footwork, and approach patterns that set up a deep first volley. Your latest session with Kimo showed better overhead contact and forehand volley reactions — keep building backhand volley feet and pace changes on high balls.
cues: Volley backhand — level face, active feet
2nd serve — spin in, keep it in
Run behind the lob, then come forward

latestDaily.label: Sat
good: Prepare higher on lob/high balls (forehand might go out); backhand hit on rise for high balls
Overhead practice made progress — hit sweet spot and better direction
Volley — some good forehands, better reaction time
bad: Volley backhand — tilting racket head, lack of foot movement at the net
Baseline ground strokes — hard time changing pace and increasing speed on high balls / lobs

good: Prepare higher on lob/high balls; backhand hit on rise
Overhead progress — sweet spot and direction
bad: Volley backhand — tilting racket head, lack of foot movement
Baseline — hard time changing pace on high balls / lobs

good: Adjust faster to new player style
Backhand take high ball on rise
bad: Hesitate to come to net — no man's zone

good: More consistent on baseline corner — corner (forehand better than backhand)
bad: Serve — too many double faults on second serves
Volley — backhand, do not cross right leg
Drop ball game — take on rise for spiny balls

sessions.context: Double game w/ Anna, Nydia, Tess, Lili
good: Return on serve more accurate — stand inside for short serves
bad: Volley mostly out — add backspin, reduce racket speed on fast balls
Consistency dropped in heat — endurance needed

good: Lower gravity center, more stable before hitting
bad: Mid court response and timing off
learning: Prepare racket high on high balls
Forced crosscourt has more success than down the line

sessions.context: Australian double w/ Kimo & Gary; rally with Seth
learning: Anticipate ball will come to you at net in doubles

bad: Top side spin 2nd serve not accurate
Volley at net long — lower legs, backspin on fast shots

sessions.context: Serving/volley solo; rally & game w/ Chen
bad: Taking ball on rise when returning

sessions.context: Double game with Lili, Anna, Nydia
learning: Doubles need strategy and communication between partners

bad: Hard time on low flat trajectories

sessions.context: Volley practice, single game, short court w/ coach
learning: Be prepared for short balls when your shot was slow
Cross court slice works on short balls

sessions.context: Practice w/ Anuchan; single game w/ Jessika
good: Good lob when opponent at net
bad: Approaching shots too fast and long
learning: Go next to ball on approaches
Overheads — go around the ball first
When serve well, come to net immediately

sessions.context: Double game with Anna, Lili, Lydia
learning: More comfortable at baseline than net
Try slice more when you get the chance
Lob + drop shot can be a weapon
Move for all shots when quality was high

Needs work:

- Volley — especially backhand
- 2nd serve — topspin + sidespin, keep it in
